title: bursera altissima
intro: Bursera altissima, also known as the Tree of Life or Oloro, is a tropical tree native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its distinctive white or yellowish flowers and its resinous bark. The resin of the Bursera altissima plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Bursera altissima can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and wounds.
